title: On the (765,192,48) Difference-Set Problem in C_3 x C_255
lean_gate_reason: Lean would currently seal only a conditional C_17 quotient-profile lemma, not a paper-ready theorem. The gating need is still a decisive exact-row proof or disproof, not formalization.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for now. The verified residue is only a conditional C_17 quotient-profile lemma, so this candidate no longer meets the micro-paper lane and should be cooled unless a same-lane exact closure appears.
family_affinity: high: exact residual-row multiplier/quotient obstruction in the multiplier-conjecture table lane
generalization_signal: moderate: the quotient-period argument should transfer to nearby rows with a verified 2-multiplier and a small prime quotient with few doubling orbits
strongest_honest_claim: Assuming a valid 2-multiplier normalization for a hypothetical (765,192,48)-difference set in C_3 x C_255, the induced C_17 quotient fiber counts are uniquely forced to satisfy m(0)=0 and m(x)=12 for every nonzero x; this does not by itself prove existence or nonexistence.
paper_title_hint: No honest standalone paper title yet. At most: A conditional C_17-quotient profile for hypothetical (765,192,48)-difference sets in C_3 x C_255.
candidate_theorem_slice: Assuming a valid 2-multiplier normalization, the C_17 quotient fiber counts are uniquely forced to the profile m(0)=0 and m(x)=12 for all nonzero x; this does not by itself contradict existence.
publication_packet_quality: thin conditional slice only; proof artifacts preserved
next_action: preserve the repaired conditional slice as a solve artifact, but move this slug aside unless a direct exact-row proof or disproof appears in the same one-shot lane; do not broaden it into a larger program
